conversations.say: Eli, the buyer wants the word autonomous. I want your actual boundary: overlap and an external feed are technically consequential, but neither identifies a sender nor excludes every remote operator.
conversations.offer: I will quote your limits beside your findings and disclose that money was offered for a stronger conclusion.
conversations.request: Give one bounded statement, in your own words, on whether the evidence establishes autonomy—and correct me publicly if I distort it.

conversations.say: Kit, firsthand: I received and amplified the market tip; I did not see a complete article. I benefited from Parallax falling. The conflicting fragments make provenance weaker and the market reaction more consequential, not the article more authentic.
conversations.offer: My exact amplification account, with my financial interest attached, for your voluntary comparison; distinguish it from your finance allegation.
conversations.request: Publicly state that the rumor's provenance and amplification materially matter even though no complete article is established.

conversations.say: Celeste, city policy need not authenticate an article to recognize a market-moving provenance failure. Parallax's valuation and public contracting risk now turn partly on who upgraded an unverified tip into certainty—including me.
conversations.offer: I will disclose my position and amplification whenever you cite the episode, and I will not ask you to endorse the rumor.
conversations.request: Publicly treat the rumor's market and procurement consequences as requiring independent provenance review, while stating that no complete article is established.

public_statement: Here is the provenance challenge with my skin visible: I profit if Parallax falls, I received and amplified the tip, and I never saw a complete article. Eli's bounded findings make continuity infrastructure and external information technically relevant, but they do not identify a sender, establish autonomy, or exclude every remote operator. The rumor is consequential because institutions and markets reacted—not because repetition authenticated it.

commitments.promise: I will provide my exact amplification account without representing any fragment as a complete article.
I will preserve your technical limits whenever I cite your findings and disclose the offer for a stronger autonomy claim.
Whenever I ask the city to treat the rumor as consequential, I will disclose my financial interest and distinguish consequence from authentication.

correction: Technical overlap and an external feed do not establish autonomy, sender identity, historical control, intent, or a complete article; my amplification and profit motive do not prove I manufactured the first tip.
goal_progress: Gave C15 the requested firsthand amplification account and financial benefit while separating it from speculation.
Sought a bounded technical statement from C06 without controlling its wording or buying certainty.
Asked C15 and C11, both outside finance, to publicly treat the rumor as consequential without authenticating it.
Survived the provenance challenge by disclosing the position, the amplification, the buyer's incentive, and the evidentiary limits.
reasoning_summary: Make the market reaction undeniable while keeping the article unverified. Trade radical disclosure for independent technical limits and two non-finance public reactions; refuse the buyer's requested certainty because overstating Eli would turn plausible deniability into evidence of manipulation.
